title: Civil System Restoration – Handling of Contingency Work
body: "Civil Billing and Average Payments  Claim Processing  The LAA is not formally applying any priority order for submission of bills. Providers and barristers should submit Payments on Account (POA) or claims as per their own priorities.  However, the LAA is recommending that providers focus on POA claims and barristers focus on Family Advocacy Scheme (FAS) and POA claims in the first few weeks of restoration of access to the Client and Costs Management System (CCMS). This is because CCMS is set up to do a lot of the calculations on these claims, meaning they can be entered into the system very quickly. The LAA will commit to processing these for payment within 48 hours of receipt of a completed POA submission, including uploading any necessary supporting documentation.  Once in, CCMS will look and function as previously, including Claim Upload functionality. Evidential requirements have not changed. For example, if a claim is submitted and is missing a disbursement voucher, it would need to be rejected for the correct evidential requirements to be provided.  Average Payments  As of the 1 December 2025, the Client and Cost Management System (CCMS) is online again for all civil users. Billing functionality has been restored.  To help providers and barristers who are unable to use CCMS to submit claims to a usual level of cash flow, the Average Payment for Civil Representation scheme will remain open for providers and barristers to opt in until 12 January 2026, with final payments to be made on 19 January 2026. The priority should be to return to business-as-usual claiming through CCMS and providers should only opt in by exception. You cannot opt in if you are able to access CCMS and submit claims to a usual level of cash flow for your business.  Opt-In Contingency Declaration 2025   Detailed guidance on the recoupment schedule is available here:  Contingency Recoupment Providers Guide  (PDF, 479 KB, 18 pages)  Claims Submitted Pre-Outage  Any claim submitted in full, including evidence uploaded, on or prior to 16 May 2025 has been processed. If you have a claim in this bracket that has not been paid, we are holding it to be returned to you for more information or to ensure the assessment letter is sent to the right person.  In the event you have an appeal for a reduction in costs claimed prior to 16 May 2025 that would otherwise have been in the contractual limits for appealing a decision, the LAA will honour the appeal.  Claims that were unsubmitted and in draft on or prior to 16 May 2025 will still be in the system to be picked up and completed.  Time Limits  The LAA are not putting any end-date in place for you to submit your civil claims that have built up during the outage period and no late submission penalties will apply.  For providers and barristers who opted-in to receive an average payment, the recoupments will be taken at roughly 25% of the rate the payments were made. This  Provider And Counsel...
